description: Add a top-level Transforms tab to the invoice template editor beside Design and Preview.
Preserve the existing Design and Preview workspace flows when the new Transforms tab is added.
Extend designer workspace/store state to represent template-level transform authoring data as first-class state.
Import existing templateAst.transforms into workspace state when a template is loaded.
Export workspace transform state back into templateAst.transforms during preview/save AST generation.
Keep templates with no transforms safe to load, edit, preview, and save without behavior changes.
Provide a source collection selector that lists any collection binding available in the template.
Show source collection metadata and discovered fields using sample/preview data when available.
Render transform pipeline cards in authored order with selection state for inspector editing.
Support adding, deleting, duplicating, and reordering transform operations in the pipeline.
Support visual authoring of filter operations.
Support visual authoring of sort operations.
Support visual authoring of group operations.
Support visual authoring of aggregate operations with one or more aggregate definitions.
Let the user author and persist a transform output binding ID.
Show a live transformed-output preview that reflects flat rows or grouped rows depending on pipeline state.
Expose grouped row fields such as key and aggregates in the output preview so users can map layout columns correctly.
Allow dynamic-table nodes to bind their source collection to the transform output binding.
Expose transformed row paths in table column mapping/binding UI for transformed and grouped outputs.
Prevent illegal transform sequences in the UI before save when the evaluator would reject them.
Surface transform-specific validation or evaluation errors inline in the Transforms tab.
Ensure authoritative preview compilation/rendering respects authored transforms.
Include the generated transforms block in the read-only AST code tab.
Preserve transform configuration through load -> edit -> save -> reopen round-trips in the visual editor.
